---
title: "Reminding myself how to use Quarto to build this website"
author: "Martin Wood"
date: "2026-05-26"
categories: [tutorial]
image: "https://quarto.org/quarto.png"
---

# Because I keep forgetting or moving machine

## Guidance links

Need these links for setting up.

Download and install, then follow the Github Pages guidance for option 2:
making use of quarto's `quarto publish` command to push rendered websites up
from the command line.

[Download Quarto](https://quarto.org/docs/download/index.html)

[Quarto (blog) setup guidance](https://quarto.org/docs/websites/website-blog.html)

[Github pages guidance](https://quarto.org/docs/publishing/github-pages.html)

## Commands

### Before publishing

```bash
quarto preview
```

Renders the website and serves it up locally so you can see what you just created 

### To render and publish up once ready

```bash
quarto render
quarto publish
```


## Squishing images

I tend to just link to images on other people's websites when discussing their software,
figure the extra networking must do their SEO some good and saves me copying copyrighted
images.

If I generate an image (2026 is weird) using just Gemini or something, it can't seem to
handle requests for lower res images or to downscale something it's produced, which is
pretty weak.

So here's how to use imagemagick to squish an image at the command line:

```bash
sudo apt install imagemagick
```

```bash
magick excel_rex_gemini.png -resize 1000x -quality 80 excel_rex_gemini_web.webp
```

The `.webp` file extension is a newfangled even more compressed format that saves
a lot on filesize so apparently just a good idea by default:  [Google WebP page](https://developers.google.com/speed/webp)

Easy to change to `.png` if suspicious.
